abstract: Abstract The use of conversational agents (e.g., chatbots) to simplify or aid consumers’ purchase decisions is on the rise. In designing those conversational agents, a key question for companies is whether and when it is advisable to enable voice-based rather than text-based interactions. Addressing this question, this study finds that matching consumers’ communication modality with product type (speaking about hedonic products; writing about utilitarian products) shapes consumers’ choice and increases choice satisfaction. Specifically, speaking fosters a feeling-based verbalizing focus, while writing triggers a reason-based focus. When this focus matches consumers’ mindset in evaluating the product type, preference fluency increases, thereby enhancing choice satisfaction. Accordingly, the authors provide insights into managing interactions with conversational agents more effectively to aid decision-making processes and increase choice satisfaction. Finally, they show that communication modality can serve as a strategic tool for low-equity brands to better compete with high-equity brands.

ob.ai_reason: 核心AI议题：会话代理chatbots与消费者决策
ob.tldr: 研究发现，对话式代理的语音或文字交互方式与产品类型相匹配（享乐产品语音，功利产品文字）能提升偏好流畅性，进而增加选择满意度，并可帮助低位品牌竞争。

title: How speaking versus writing to conversational agents shapes consumers’ choice and choice satisfaction
title_zh: 对话式Agent的语音交互与书面交互对消费者选择及选择满意度的影响
